---
summary: 'Bundled, pre-built apps you clone-and-deploy from the desktop gallery.'
title: 'Templates'
---

# Templates

Templates are pre-built Centraid apps that ship with the desktop. Each one is **identical in shape** to an app you'd author yourself — `index.html`, `app.css`, `app.js`, `queries/`, `actions/`, `migrations/`, `app.json`. You clone, you tweak, you ship.

The templates live in [`packages/blueprints/`](https://github.com/srikanthsrungarapu/centraid/tree/main/packages/blueprints) (the package that owns "how a new app comes into being" — blank scaffolders, template clone, and this gallery).

## The two flavors

Templates are split by **kind**, which decides both the directory they live in and the gallery tab they surface under.

### Plain apps (`kind: 'app'`)

Single-purpose UIs that live under `apps/<id>/`. They carry an `index.html` and front-end assets.

| Template  | What it is                                                                 |
| --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `hydrate` | Track 8 cups a day. One table, one query, one action. The reference shape. |
| `todos`   | Capture and clear small things.                                            |
| `journal` | A clean place to write each day.                                           |

### Automation apps (`kind: 'automation'`)

Headless apps that live under `automations/<id>/`: an `app.json` with `kind: "automation"` and empty `actions[]` / `queries[]`, **no `index.html`**, and one automation under `automations/<id>/{automation.json, handler.js}`. The handler typically gathers data via `ctx.tool`, calls `ctx.agent` to reason, and writes the result back. See the [Automations](/automations/index) tab for the runtime.

| Template                  | Category      | Trigger                    |
| ------------------------- | ------------- | -------------------------- |
| `briefing`                | Daily rhythm  | Weekdays · 6:00 PM         |
| `evening-wrap-up`         | Daily rhythm  | Daily · 9:00 PM            |
| `meeting-prep`            | Inbox & comms | Weekdays · 8:45 AM         |
| `email-triage`            | Inbox & comms | Weekdays · 8:30 AM         |
| `issue-triage`            | Reliability   | Weekdays · 9:00 AM         |
| `pr-review-digest`        | Engineering   | Weekdays · 11:30 PM        |
| `release-notes-drafter`   | Engineering   | On merge to main (webhook) |
| `dependency-update-check` | Engineering   | Tuesdays · 12:00 AM        |
| `flaky-test-tracker`      | Reliability   | Mondays · 9:30 AM          |
| `system-health-check`     | Reliability   | Daily · 5:30 PM            |

Template ids are **not** prefixed (`briefing`, not `auto.briefing`) — the kind alone routes them.

## What's inside `packages/blueprints/`

```
packages/blueprints/
├── README.md
├── src/                      # listTemplates(), resolveTemplates(), fetchRemoteTemplates(), clone, scaffolders
├── index.json                # hand-edited manifest source — TemplateManifest
├── manifest.json             # generated by scripts/build-manifest.mjs (checked in)
├── apps/<id>/                # one folder per UI-app template (kind: 'app')
│   ├── hydrate/
│   ├── todos/
│   └── journal/
└── automations/<id>/         # one folder per automation template (kind: 'automation')
    ├── briefing/
    ├── evening-wrap-up/
    └── …
```

Each `<id>/` directory is a fully-formed app. `index.json` is the **gallery manifest source**; `scripts/build-manifest.mjs` walks it plus each template folder to emit `manifest.json` (the runtime catalog, with each template's `files[]` enumerated and `app.json#knobs` folded in as `appKnobs`).

The kind-segment directory (`apps/` vs `automations/`) is **derived from `kind`**, never stored separately — the manifest build, the on-disk resolver (`templateSourceDir`), and the remote fetcher all compute it the same way (`templateKindDir`).

### Gallery manifest entry

For a plain app — `kind` is optional and defaults to `'app'`:

```json
{
  "id": "hydrate",
  "name": "Hydrate",
  "desc": "Track 8 cups a day.",
  "colorKey": "indigo",
  "iconKey": "Water",
  "version": "0.1.0"
}
```

For an automation — `kind` plus the richer card fields:

```json
{
  "id": "briefing",
  "name": "Briefing",
  "desc": "A scannable catch-up on your calendar, inbox, and messages.",
  "colorKey": "rose",
  "iconKey": "Sparkle",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "kind": "automation",
  "emoji": "🌤",
  "category": "Daily rhythm",
  "triggerKind": "cron",
  "triggerLabel": "Weekdays · 6:00 PM",
  "integrations": ["Google Calendar", "Gmail", "Slack"]
}
```

The `kind` / `triggerKind` / `triggerLabel` / `integrations` fields drive how the gallery renders automations versus apps, and which tab they appear under. This is **gallery-level metadata** — `app.json` inside the template directory carries its own runtime `kind`, but the gallery card fields (`emoji`, `category`, `triggerLabel`, …) live only on the `index.json` entry.

## Pre-built handlers

Templates ship with their handlers **already compiled to `.js`** — only the runtime `.js` is committed (no `.ts` source, no `tsconfig.json`). Cloning is "click and deploy": the gateway loads `.js` directly, with no `bun install` and no compile step on first use.

## The remote resolver

The bundled catalog is the floor, not the ceiling. The gateway resolves **bundle-or-cache** per template, preferring the higher semver version (`resolveTemplates`). When a remote manifest URL is configured, `fetchRemoteTemplates` pulls newer templates into a per-gateway cache dir in the background; it never throws (offline / 404 / bad manifest leave the cache untouched), so the gallery always degrades cleanly to the bundle. Because the checked-in tree doubles as the remote tree, a GitHub-raw URL serving `manifest.json` + `<apps|automations>/<id>/<file>` works as a remote source unchanged.
